posture_break.name: Posture Break
posture_break.definition: Breaking opponent's upright alignment from guard — pulling head/shoulders below their hips to neutralize their base and strikes.
posture_break.body_concept: Pull collar or head down, close distance between your chest and theirs. Their hands go to the mat = vulnerability.
reinforced_by: Any closed guard class — this is the root of all bottom attacks

mount_stabilization.name: Mount Stabilization
mount_stabilization.definition: Maintaining mount against bridging and shrimping — keeping hips heavy, base wide, reacting to escape attempts.
mount_stabilization.body_concept: Grapevines or heels on hips. Knees squeeze. Hips heavy. Hands post when they bridge. Ride the wave.
reinforced_by: Every class that ends in mount — practice stabilization before submission

crossface.name: Crossface
crossface.definition: Shoulder pressure across opponent's jaw/face to control their head direction and deny them the underhook or hip escape.
crossface.body_concept: Shoulder blade drives into far cheek. Their head turns away = they cannot face you = they cannot re-guard.
reinforced_by: Side control classes, half guard passing

underhook.name: Underhook
underhook.definition: Arm threaded under opponent's arm from inside, controlling their shoulder. Whoever wins the underhook controls the position.
underhook.body_concept: Elbow tight, hand on their back/shoulder blade. Head fights to the underhook side. Creates a frame they cannot break.
reinforced_by: Half guard (both sides), butterfly guard, clinch work

seatbelt.name: Seatbelt Grip
seatbelt.definition: Chest-to-back control with one arm over the shoulder and one under the armpit, hands clasped across opponent's chest.
seatbelt.body_concept: Over-arm controls the choking lane. Under-arm prevents them turning in. Chest glued to their back — zero space.
reinforced_by: Any back control class — seatbelt is the root grip

hip_escape.name: Hip Escape (Shrimp)
hip_escape.definition: Moving hips away from opponent to create space or change angle. The most fundamental defensive and offensive movement in BJJ.
hip_escape.body_concept: Bridge onto shoulder → slide hips away → face opponent. Used to escape, re-guard, set up sweeps, and create angles for submissions.
reinforced_by: Guard recovery drills, mount escape, side control escape — appears everywhere

bridge.name: Bridge (Upa)
bridge.definition: Explosive hip elevation to off-balance or escape from bottom. The primary mount escape mechanism.
bridge.body_concept: Feet flat, drive hips to ceiling. Direction matters — bridge over one shoulder toward trapped arm side.

collar_grip.name: Collar Grip
collar_grip.definition: Deep grip inside the gi collar, controlling the opponent's posture and setting up chokes and sweeps.
collar_grip.body_concept: Four fingers inside collar, deep past the label. Elbow stays tight. Pull down and toward you for posture break; cross-grip for choke.
reinforced_by: Gi-specific classes — collar grip is the gi equivalent of clinch control

figure_four_lock.name: Figure-Four Lock
figure_four_lock.definition: Interlocking grip where your wrist controls their wrist and your other hand grabs your own wrist — creates a lever system.
figure_four_lock.body_concept: Appears in kimura, americana, armbar grip, RNC. Same grip shape, different application angles.
reinforced_by: Any class teaching americana, kimura, or armbar finish mechanics

arm_isolation.name: Arm Isolation
arm_isolation.definition: Separating one of opponent's arms from their body to attack it — the prerequisite for armbar, americana, kimura.
arm_isolation.body_concept: Two-on-one control. Pin at wrist or elbow. Create separation between their arm and their torso.

hook_insertion.name: Hook Insertion
hook_insertion.definition: Placing insteps (hooks) inside opponent's thighs from back control. Prevents them from sliding down to escape.
hook_insertion.body_concept: Bottom hook first (bearing their weight). Top hook second. Heels dig inward, not down. If they peel hooks, transition to body triangle.
reinforced_by: Back control classes — hooks are the retention system

knee_slice.name: Knee Slice Pass
knee_slice.definition: Passing guard by sliding the knee across opponent's thigh while controlling their upper body, clearing the guard leg.
knee_slice.body_concept: Cross-face controls head. Knee slides across midline. Shin pins their thigh. Hip drops to clear. Underhook the far side on landing.
reinforced_by: Half guard passing classes, guard passing fundamentals

off_balance.name: Off-Balancing (Kuzushi)
off_balance.definition: Disrupting opponent's base before attempting a sweep or throw. Without kuzushi, no sweep works.
off_balance.body_concept: Push-pull. Load their weight onto one side, then sweep the other. They must be falling BEFORE you sweep.
reinforced_by: Every sweep class — kuzushi is the universal setup

guard_retention.name: Guard Retention
guard_retention.definition: Keeping opponent in your guard when they attempt to pass — using frames, hip movement, and leg pummeling to re-establish guard.
guard_retention.body_concept: Frames on biceps/collar. Hips never flat. Knees track their hips. If they pass one leg, re-pummel the other.
reinforced_by: Open guard classes, guard recovery drills

angle_cutting.name: Angle Cutting
angle_cutting.definition: Turning perpendicular to opponent to tighten a triangle, armbar, or create a sweep angle. The difference between a loose and tight submission.
angle_cutting.body_concept: Pivot on your shoulders. Walk your hips to a 45-90° angle from opponent's centerline. Their arm/head is now trapped at maximum leverage.

weight_distribution.name: Weight Distribution
weight_distribution.definition: Controlling where your weight presses on the opponent — the invisible skill that separates heavy from light top pressure.
weight_distribution.body_concept: Shift weight into chest/shoulder contact point. Remove weight from hands and feet — they should be light. Opponent feels 200% of your weight on one spot.
reinforced_by: Side control, mount, knee on belly classes

back_take_timing.name: Back Take Timing
back_take_timing.definition: Recognizing when opponent turns away (exposing their back) and immediately transitioning to back control before they complete the turn.
back_take_timing.body_concept: When they turn away: seatbelt FIRST, then chase hips, then insert hooks. Never hooks before seatbelt — you'll get shaken off.
reinforced_by: Side control → back take transitions, turtle top work

grip_fighting.name: Grip Fighting
grip_fighting.definition: Establishing your grips while stripping theirs — the standing and guard-pulling meta-game.
grip_fighting.body_concept: Get your grips first. If they grip first, strip immediately — two-on-one break, circle wrist, push elbow. Never let them settle grips.
reinforced_by: Standing classes, guard pulling, open guard

opponent_reading.name: Opponent Reading
opponent_reading.definition: Recognizing opponent's defensive reaction and choosing the correct attack branch — the core skill of network roads.
opponent_reading.body_concept: If they protect the neck: attack the arm. If they protect the arm: attack the neck. If they bridge: ride and re-attack. Read, don't guess.
reinforced_by: Positional sparring with specific attack/defense roles
